rad_findings_en:
Subpleural ground-glass nodule measuring 7 mm in the left lower lobe (series 7, image 23). Mild bronchiectasis in the subpleural portions of the left lower lobe. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy. Diverticulum along the posterior right wall of the trachea measuring up to 1.1 cm × 9 mm × 1 mm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. No wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included portions of the abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en:
1.

Ground-glass nodule in the left lower lobe. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, CT in 6–12 months to confirm persistence, then CT every 2 years until 5 years.

2.

Mild bronchiectasis of the left lower lobe.

3.

Tracheal diverticulum.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Against a background of gravitational (dependent) changes, there are subpleural ground-glass opacities in the left lung base, a few subpleural ground-glass opacities in the lingular segment. Mild bronchiectasis in the subpleural portions of the left lower lobe and lingular segment. No pulmonary nodules require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy. Diverticulum along the posterior right wall of the trachea measures up to 1.1 cm × 9 mm × 1 mm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. No wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included portions of the abdomen: Please see separately dictated abdomen CT, performed concurrently.
val_impression_en:
1. Subpleural ground-glass opacities in the left lung base and in the lingular segment with small bronchiectasis may be an inflammatory / infectious disease.

2. Tracheal diverticulum.

3.  No pulmonary nodules require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Both lungs are fully expanded and abut the chest wall throughout.
No areas of interstitial change or consolidation are identified.
Solid nodules in segment 6 of the right lung and in segment 6 of the left lung, subcortical, measuring up to 6 mm.
The central tracheobronchial tree is typically developed.
The lumen of the trachea and main bronchi is visualized, without abnormal bronchial contents.

Mediastinum and hila: Not displaced. No mass is identified. Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

Heart: The heart is in the typical location and is enlarged in transverse dimension due to increased size of the cardiac chambers. Moderate coronary calcification.
No abnormal fluid is identified in the pericardial space.

Great vessels: The aorta measures up to 3.3 cm in the ascending portion, 3.0 cm at the level of the arch, and up to 2.6 cm in the descending portion. Aortic wall calcification.
The pulmonary trunk measures up to 3.7 cm.
A catheter is present in the right internal jugular vein, with the tip in the right atrium.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate spondylosis of the thoracic spine.

Included upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en:
1. No CT evidence of inflammatory changes in the lungs.

2. Solid nodules in segment 6 of the right lung and in segment 6 of the left lung, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes.

3. Cardiac chamber enlargement. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Moderate coronary calcification.

4. Moderate spondylosis of the thoracic spine.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Incomplete breath, pronounced gravitational changes. No consolidation is identified. Small perifissural nodes measure up to 2 mm, require no follow up.
Solid nodules in the lower right lobe measure up to 4 mm (series 5, images 79, 91, 102, 148), some of the nodules may be gravitational changes. 
Solid nodules in the lower left lobe measure up to 4 mm (series 5, images 94, 108, 127, 134), some of the nodules may be gravitational changes. 
Solid nodules in the upper left lobe measure up to 3 mm (series 5, images 67, 97, 102). 
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and hila: No mass or adenopathy is identified. 

Heart: The heart is enlarged due to increased size of the ventricles, cardiothoracic ratio 0.55. Moderate coronary calcification. Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves. No significant pericardial effusion.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Mild aortic wall calcification. The pulmonary trunk measures up to 3.7 cm. A catheter is present in the right internal jugular vein, with the tip in the right atrium.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate spondylosis of the thoracic spine. Subchondral cysts of the left shoulder joint.

Included upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodules of both lungs measure up to 4 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Mild cardiomegaly. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (37 mm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, degenerative changes of the left shoulder joint.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Micronodules in the right middle lobe and left upper lobe measure up to 3 mm. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the spine.

Upper abdomen: In the medial limb of the left adrenal gland, there is a lesion measuring 1.4 × 1.2 cm on axial images with macroscopic fat within the lesion, likely an adenoma. Cholecystectomy.
rad_impression_en:
1.

Bilateral pulmonary micronodules. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: Generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2.

Left adrenal lesion. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign adenoma. No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

3.

Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

4.

Cholecystectomy.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the upper lobe of the left lung, a ground glass area is identified adjacent to the interlobar pleura. In the upper lobe of the left lung, solid nodules are determined against the background of ground glass as follows: 
- in the image 22, series 5, a nodule measures 2 mm.
- in the image 28, series 5, a subpleural nodule measures 2 mm.
- in the image 29, series 5, a perifissural nodule measures 3 mm, likely a perifissural node.
- in the image 31, series 5, a subpleural nodule measures 2 mm.
- in the image 38, series 5, a perifissural nodule measures 3 mm, likely a perifissural node.
In other lobes of the lungs there are a few scattered micronodules of 2-3 mm. No consolidation or pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the spine.

Upper abdomen: In the medial limb of the left adrenal gland, there is a lesion measuring 1.4 × 1.2 cm, -13 HU, likely an adenoma. Cholecystectomy.
val_impression_en:
1. In the upper lobe of the left lung, a ground glass area and multiple small solid nodules may be residual changes after an inflammatory/infectious process. A few scattered micronodules of both lungs. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Left adrenal lesion. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign adenoma. No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:

Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule measuring 6 mm along the minor interlobar fissure on image 107 (no change).

In the subpleural portions of both lower lobes, there are extensive areas of ground-glass opacity, series 5, images 130-160 (increased in size; previously at these levels there were plate-like atelectasis). In the right middle lobe and both lower lobes, there are plate-like atelectasis. In the right lower lobe, there are single cysts measuring up to 4 mm, series 5, images 119, 144 (no change). No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Coronary stent placement.

Great vessels: Dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta up to 4.0 cm, mild wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included abdomen: In liver segment 4, a hypodense round lesion measuring up to 5 mm (no change). Colonic diverticula.
rad_impression_en:
1. Ground-glass opacities in both lower lobes, increased (may correspond to inflammatory changes). Perifissural lymph node in the right lower lobe (no further CT follow-up is required per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations). Plate-like atelectasis in both lungs.

2. Dilatation of the ascending aorta.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Coronary stent placement.

4. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

6. Hypodense liver lesion (per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017, no additional evaluation is required).

7. Colonic diverticula.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the right lower lobe, perifissural node measures 6 mm along the minor interlobar fissure (series 5, image 107), unchanged, requires no follow up. In the right lower lobe, there are single cysts measuring up to 4 mm, (series 5, images 119, 144), unchanged. Atelectasis (ground-glass areas) is detected supradiaphragmatically and in the lung bases; the appearance of atelectasis changed during the studies from January 26, 2026 and March 14, 2025, an increase in size is noted. Gravitational changes dorsally. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: Enlargement of the ventricles. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Coronary stent. Fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles.

Great vessels: Dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta up to 4.0 cm, mild wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included abdomen: Please see separately dictated abdomen CT, performed concurrently.
val_impression_en:
1. Ground-glass opacities (atelectasis) in both lower lobes, increased, may correspond to interstitial changes/scarring. Perifissural lymph node in the right lower lobe (no further CT follow-up is required per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations). 

2. Dilatation of the ascending aorta up to 4.0 cm, mild wall calcification. 

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Coronary stent. Enlargement of the ventricles. Fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles may correspond to consequence of atherosclerotic heart disease.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:  
Solitary solid nodules are present in the right lung, in series 5 as follows:  
— In the upper lobe:  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 4 mm (slice 214), calcified.  
* Nodule measuring up to 3 mm (slice 62), calcified.  
In the left lung, a solitary micronodule up to 2 mm is present in the upper lobe (slice 49).  
No pleural effusion.  
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesion or lymphadenopathy.  
The cardiac portion and part of the stomach body herniate into the posterior mediastinum, forming a hiatal hernia: diaphragmatic crural diastasis about 2.6 cm, hernia about 5.7 × 4.8 cm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion with a layer thickness of about 9 mm.  
Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.  
Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, osteophytes at the C6-C7 level. A small focus of bone lucency deforming the anterior cortical layer of the xiphoid process measures up to 4 mm (series 4, slice 132).

Included portions of the abdomen: Thickening of the left adrenal gland up to 9 mm.

Thyroid gland: A nodular structure of similar density abuts the inferior margin of the left lobe, measuring about 1.9 × 1.3 cm (series 4, slice 3).
rad_impression_en:
1. Solitary pulmonary nodules. Per Fleischner Society 2017 recommendations, no additional CT follow-up is required.  

2. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Small pericardial effusion.  

3. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.  

4. Hiatal hernia.  

5. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine and cervical osteophytes within the imaged field. Bone lucency focus in the xiphoid process is too small for assessment. Bone-RADS 3, CT follow-up in 6 months.  

6. Nodular structure at the inferior margin of the left thyroid lobe on this noncontrast study, of unclear etiology (accessory thyroid tissue versus supraclavicular lymph node). Correlate with ultrasound.  

7. Thickening of the left adrenal gland. Per Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the American College of Radiology Incidental Findings Committee, 2017, CT follow-up is not required.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Solitary solid nodules are present in the right lung, in series 5 as follows:  
* In the upper lobe, subpleural calcified nodule measures up to 4 mm (slice 38).  
* In the upper lobe, calcified nodule measures up to 3 mm (slice 62).  
In the left lung, a solitary micronodule up to 2 mm is present in the upper lobe (slice 49).  
No pleural effusion. The trachea and main bronchi are patent. Thickening of the walls of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. The cardiac portion, fundus, and part of the stomach body herniate into the posterior mediastinum, forming a hiatal hernia: diaphragmatic crural diastasis about 2.6 cm, hernia about 5.7 × 4.8 cm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion is identified. Against the background of pulsation artifacts in the area of the left anterior descending artery, a single small hyperdense area is identified.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Mild wall calcification of the left common carotid artery and abdominal aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, osteophytes at the C6-C7 level. Sternal foramen is clinically insignificant, developmental variant.

Included portions of the abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. Calcified pulmonary nodules, healed granulomas. Per Fleischner Society 2017 recommendations, no additional CT follow-up is required.  

2. Hiatal hernia, likely clinically significant.

3. Likely mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. 

4. Mild wall calcification of the left common carotid artery and abdominal aorta.
